biomechanicalMeaning: Size of patellofemoral contact loading.
limitations: Model and contact-area assumptions remain condition specific.

biomechanicalMeaning: Force-time development across the event.
limitations: Peak values cannot substitute for impulse or rate.

biomechanicalMeaning: Knee-flexion and task geometry exposing PFJ contact.
limitations: Context is not injury probability or magnitude.

biomechanicalMeaning: Size of tibiofemoral compression or shear.
limitations: Intersegmental resultants must not be labeled compartment contact force.

biomechanicalMeaning: Compression or shear development over an event.
limitations: Direction and event windows must match.

biomechanicalMeaning: Directional and compartment context for tibiofemoral loading.
limitations: Context does not convert an intersegmental force to contact force.

biomechanicalMeaning: Peak patellar-tendon force or strain.
limitations: Force and strain scales are separate comparison families.

biomechanicalMeaning: Force-time or source-defined average strain profile.
limitations: Event average must not be encoded as peak.

biomechanicalMeaning: Knee geometry and loading setup exposing the tendon.
limitations: Context cannot infer force without a validated relation.

biomechanicalMeaning: Peak quadriceps-tendon force or strain.
limitations: Geometry and model assumptions are source bounded.

biomechanicalMeaning: Duration and development of tendon exposure.
limitations: Duration requires phase segmentation.

biomechanicalMeaning: Deep-flexion and patellar force-transmission geometry.
limitations: Context remains separate from magnitude.

biomechanicalMeaning: Peak ACL strain or modeled force.
limitations: Kinematic risk factors are not magnitude measurements.

biomechanicalMeaning: Timing and rate of ACL exposure.
limitations: Timing reference must be explicit.

biomechanicalMeaning: Structured anterior-shear and rotational exposure context.
limitations: Landing task alone does not establish ligament strain.

biomechanicalMeaning: Peak PCL strain or modeled ligament force.
limitations: Posterior translation is context unless directly measured and validated.

biomechanicalMeaning: Deep-flexion exposure duration or strain rate.
limitations: No impulse substitution without target evidence.

biomechanicalMeaning: Knee-flexion and posterior-shear mechanism context.
limitations: Context must not replace modeled ligament force.

biomechanicalMeaning: Peak meniscal or cartilage contact magnitude.
limitations: Complex estimates do not overwrite tissue-specific evidence.

biomechanicalMeaning: Pressure-time and contact-duration profile.
limitations: Pressure and force-time families remain distinct.

biomechanicalMeaning: Deep-flexion and rotation context for compartment exposure.
limitations: Complex context is a fallback only.

biomechanicalMeaning: Peak hamstring tissue force or strain.
limitations: Muscle and tendon outputs remain separate when available.

biomechanicalMeaning: Lengthening rate and eccentric exposure.
limitations: Metric families are not interchangeable.

biomechanicalMeaning: Joint-angle and movement-phase context for lengthening demand.
limitations: Dynamic stabilization is represented separately.

biomechanicalMeaning: Magnitude of dynamic anterior-shear or rotational stabilization demand.
limitations: Not a tissue force score.

biomechanicalMeaning: Timing of dynamic stabilization response.
limitations: Not cumulative tissue exposure.

biomechanicalMeaning: Task context requiring dynamic hamstring stabilization.
limitations: Kept separate from tissue mechanical load.

biomechanicalMeaning: Peak Achilles force or strain.
limitations: Force and strain require separate scales.

biomechanicalMeaning: Force-time or source-defined average-strain profile.
limitations: Event average is not peak.

biomechanicalMeaning: Ankle task and loading context governing Achilles exposure.
limitations: Protocol identities cannot be inherited through source ID alone.

biomechanicalMeaning: Peak talocrural compression or directional force.
limitations: Joint contact and intersegmental resultants remain distinct.

biomechanicalMeaning: Joint force-time development across the event.
limitations: Event boundaries and directions must match.

biomechanicalMeaning: Ankle angle and landing context for talocrural exposure.
limitations: Context does not establish internal force.

biomechanicalMeaning: Peak lateral-ankle ligament strain or force.
limitations: ATFL CFL and PTFL claims remain separate when direct.

biomechanicalMeaning: Rate and timing of ligament exposure.
limitations: Peak strain cannot substitute for rate.

biomechanicalMeaning: Position and perturbation context for passive lateral stability.
limitations: Peroneal stabilization is separate.

biomechanicalMeaning: Magnitude of dynamic eversion-response demand.
limitations: Not lateral-ligament force.

biomechanicalMeaning: Latency and delay of peroneal stabilization.
limitations: Timing reference must be explicit.

biomechanicalMeaning: Context requiring active peroneal stabilization.
limitations: Kept separate from passive ligament load.

biomechanicalMeaning: Peak syndesmotic strain force or separation.
limitations: Cadaveric and finite-element scope must remain explicit.

biomechanicalMeaning: Rate and timing of syndesmotic exposure.
limitations: No generic transfer across protocols.

biomechanicalMeaning: External-rotation and axial-load context for the syndesmosis.
limitations: Context alone cannot assign strain.

biomechanicalMeaning: Magnitude of medial ligament tendon or arch-support exposure.
limitations: FDL and FHL require explicit support.

biomechanicalMeaning: Duration of medial-arch and tendon exposure.
limitations: Duration requires a defined arch or force threshold.

biomechanicalMeaning: Pronation eversion and support context for medial stability.
limitations: Complex context is not tissue-specific force.

biomechanicalMeaning: Magnitude of active medial-arch stabilization demand.
limitations: Not posterior-tibial tendon force.

biomechanicalMeaning: Timing and duration of active arch stabilization.
limitations: Not cumulative tendon exposure.

biomechanicalMeaning: Context requiring active posterior-tibial stabilization.
limitations: Kept separate from tendon mechanical load.
